codeReview.summary: The feature mostly implements the requested TSV-aware voter enrichment and adds strong targeted test coverage, but there is one blocking correctness regression: business-entity detection now skips legitimate person names containing INC/CORP substrings (e.g., VINCENT, LINCOLN), causing voter enrichment to be incorrectly bypassed for those leads.

issues.description: Business-entity skipping uses is_business_entity(owner), but is_business_entity performs substring matching for keywords like "INC" and "CORP". This incorrectly classifies personal names such as "VINCENT SMITH" and "LINCOLN JONES" as business entities, so enrich_with_voter_data() skips valid human leads and fails to populate contact_phone/contact_email. This violates expected behavior for person-lead enrichment while attempting to satisfy the business-entity skip requirement.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The voter_lookup module now supports TSV and Florida Division extract header variants, but its module docstring and missing-file diagnostic still state only CSV input. Shared docs do not capture this broadened accepted format.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: scrapers/voter_lookup.py line 12 says "Format: CSV" and line 258 says "place FL voter roll CSV" even though load_voter_roll() now auto-detects TSV via first-line delimiter at lines 161-164.

sharedStateObservations.observation: There is no documented convention for when to use strict word-boundary business-entity detection vs loose substring detection in owner-name workflows. Reusing the broad Sunbiz helper in voter enrichment introduced false positives on person names.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: scrapers/sunbiz.py lines 31-40 define is_business_entity via substring checks; scrapers/voter_lookup.py line 268 applies it directly to skip enrichment. Quick check in this environment shows is_business_entity('VINCENT SMITH') and is_business_entity('LINCOLN JONES') both return True.

summary: Reviewed feature voter-pipeline-infrastructure via feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff, and shared-state docs. TSV/CSV detection, name+address matching, missing-file fallback, and test additions are present, but the business-entity skip logic introduces a blocking false-positive regression for real person names containing INC/CORP substrings, so this review fails.
